title: Tournefortia aubletii
taxonomy: Tournefortia aubletii is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It belongs to the genus Tournefortia and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of Tournefortia aubletii is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Boraginales, family Boraginaceae, genus Tournefortia, species T. aubletii. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its leaf shape and flower structure. By understanding its classification, scientists can better study and conserve this unique species.

intro: Tournefortia aubletii, also known as Tournefortia, is a tropical plant species native to Central and South America, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and woody, branching stems. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, respiratory infections, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Tournefortia aubletii include diterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Tournefortia is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the form of essential oil or tincture to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
